title: Insight
description: A pattern surfaced to the user. Observational, not interpretive (idea.md §5.1). Insights surface in language drawn from the user's own utterances with retrievable evidence.

insight_id.description: Stable ID. Used to dedupe and to track user response over time.

type.description: idea.md §5.4. Pattern: X after Y, N times in M days. Echo: surfaced past utterance. Shift: talking about X differently. Context: feelings around time/place/situation. Each has its own rendering template.

rendered_text.description: Final user-facing copy. Generated to mirror the user's register. No clinical jargon. No interpretation — just observation.

evidence.description: Backs the 'tap to see what this is based on' affordance (idea.md §5.1). Every insight MUST cite verifiable evidence from the user's data.

excerpt.description: Verbatim or near-verbatim excerpt. For echoes, this IS the surfaced text.

confidence.description: Confidence in the pattern. Surfacing thresholds vary by insight type and user's intensity setting.

min_intensity.description: Lowest user intensity setting at which this insight is eligible to surface. Quiet-tier users see this only on explicit ask.

data_density_check.description: Verifies the data-density gate (idea.md §5.2). v1: 3+ co-occurrences AND 2+ weeks of use for graduated insights.

cc_anchored.description: True if this insight is grounded in the user's stated chief complaint (idea.md §5.2). Initial weeks: only cc-anchored insights surface.

correction_note.description: User-provided 'this isn't right' feedback. Feeds back into the state document.
